page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. Operating at 2.8 GHz, this instance provides 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, utilizing a 1:1 core-to-thread ratio without hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It features 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The server operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, has a baseline network bandwidth of 67 Gbps, and does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gb.8xlarge exhibits strong memory performance, with uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 386.95 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 184.61 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. The Passmark memory score and database operations also rank in the top tier, with database operations scoring 47,436.59. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, as demonstrated by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 21,327.0 and strong software compilation performance. However, single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores remain in the average tier. For language model tasks, the instance achieves a strong 28.94 tokens/sec for medium 7B model text generation, though text generation for smaller 135M models is relatively poor.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation deliver high resource density and exceptional memory throughput without the overhead of hyperthreading. This makes the instance highly suitable for memory-bound and multi-threaded workloads. The server is well-suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and redis-based caching layers, where its high memory bandwidth and balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio can be fully leveraged.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by 32 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 67 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks highlight top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached reads at 386.95 GB/sec and writes at 184.61 GB/sec, alongside top-tier database operations and multi-core Geekbench performance. This hardware profile is highly efficient for multi-threaded, memory-intensive workloads. It is optimized for database hosting, software compilation, Redis caching, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, 67 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier memory performance.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and dedicated core performance for database and multi-threaded workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier memory read and write bandwidth for uncached large blocks
67 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving
